metadata.task: Set a disk in motion by applying a torque of 20 N·m on its central axis. First, calculate its angular velocity and rotational energy after 0.2 real life seconds of simulation. Then, apply another torque of 10 N·m onto the same disk on its central axis again and calculate the new angular velocity and rotational energy after 0.2 real life seconds. Round to the nearest tenth if needed.

expected_behavior: The LLM should apply a torque to rotate the object and calculate the angular momentum and rotational energy after 2 seconds of simulation.
reasoning: By applying torque, the object will rotate and accumulate angular momentum. The rotational energy can be calculated using the formula for kinetic energy of rotation.
